[Phyphox wikipedia: Acceleration(without g)](https://phyphox.org/wiki/index.php/Sensor:_Acceleration_(without_g))

# Import Data

The code below imports a .csv file exported from **Phyphox**.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(zoo)

accel <- read_csv("data/acceleration/Raw Data.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  rename(
    time = `Time (s)`,
    x = `Linear Acceleration x (m/s^2)`,
    y = `Linear Acceleration y (m/s^2)`,
    z = `Linear Acceleration z (m/s^2)`,
    abs = "Absolute acceleration (m/s^2)"
  )

accel |>
  head() |>
  kable()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


|      time|         x|          y|         z|       abs|
|---------:|---------:|----------:|---------:|---------:|
| 0.0134303| 0.1704626|  0.0253725| 0.8257484| 0.8435412|
| 0.0184303| 0.3978221|  0.0507278| 1.7292604| 1.7751556|
| 0.0234303| 0.6281593|  0.0596471| 2.4590082| 2.5386735|
| 0.0284303| 0.8147621|  0.0321317| 2.7522707| 2.8705163|
| 0.0334303| 0.8607306| -0.1388440| 2.5067110| 2.6540036|
| 0.0384303| 0.6844928| -0.3706989| 1.9562540| 2.1054401|


:::
:::


# Visualisation

Let's start out with some simple graphs.

## Absolute Acceleration


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
accel <- accel |>
  filter(abs < 14) # remove outliers

ggplot(accel, aes(x = time, y = abs)) +
  geom_jitter(color = "#0ff0c0") +
  labs(title = "Abs. Acceleration vs Time", x = "Time (s)", y = "Absolute Acceleration (m/s^2)") +
  theme_bw()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Linear Acceleration


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(accel, aes(x = time, y = x, color = abs)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.05, se = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation:
colour.
ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in
  the data.
ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical
  variable into a factor?
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(accel, aes(x = time, y = y)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.05, se = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-2.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(accel, aes(x = time, y = z)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.05, se = FALSE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Summary data:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
accel_summary <- tibble(mean_x = mean(accel$x), mean_y = mean(accel$y), mean_z = mean(accel$z))

accel_summary
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 3
  mean_x  mean_y    mean_z
   <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl>
1 0.0138 -0.0955 -0.000834
```


:::
:::
